description_long: This is a large, amphora-style vase with a wide mouth, flared rim, and two robust handles extending from the neck to the shoulder. The body is decorated with intricate black-figure artwork depicting mythological scenes, likely inspired by ancient Greek pottery. The surface features a terracotta base color with black painted figures and ornamental motifs, including floral and geometric patterns around the neck and handles. The vase has a rounded belly tapering to a narrow foot, providing stability. The interior appears dark, suggesting a glazed or painted finish, while the exterior is matte with some glossy highlights on the painted areas. The craftsmanship and detailed iconography indicate it is both decorative and functional, suitable for display in a refined urban or cultural setting.
description: A large, amphora-style vase with black-figure mythological scenes and ornate handles, finished in terracotta and black. The vase features intricate decorative motifs and a wide, flared rim.
description_view_0: Front view showing detailed black-figure artwork of human figures and ornate floral patterns around the neck.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one of the handles and symmetrical black ornamental motifs with less narrative detail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying another mythological scene with multiple figures and a clear view of the vase's rounded belly.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the second handle and matching ornamental motifs, with partial figure artwork visible.
attribute_pattern: mythological black-figure scenes with floral and geometric motifs
